'''Craig Woodworth Holden''' was the finance department chair and Gregg T. and Judith A. Summerville Chair of Finance<ref>{{Cite web|title=Faculty Profile|url=https://kelley.iu.edu/faculty-research/faculty-directory/profile.cshtml|access-date=2020-06-22|website=Kelley School of Business|language=en}}</ref> at the [[Kelley School of Business]] at [[Indiana University]]. His research focused on [[market microstructure]]. 
==Biography==
Holden received his M.B.A. and Ph.D. from the [[UCLA Anderson School of Management|Anderson School of Management]] at [[UCLA]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Dissertations UCLA Anderson School of Management|work=UCLA Anderson School of Management|url=http://www.anderson.ucla.edu/degrees/phd/dissertations|access-date=21 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141129081342/http://www.anderson.ucla.edu/degrees/phd/dissertations|archive-date=29 November 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> 
He received the [[Fama-DFA Prize]] for the second best paper in capital markets published in the ''[[Journal of Financial Economics]]'' in 2009,<ref>{{cite web |url=http://jfe.rochester.edu/winners.htm |title=Fama-DFA Prizes for the Best Papers Published in the Journal of Financial Economics in the Areas of Capital Markets and Asset Pricing |work=jfe.rochester.edu |year=2012 |access-date=March 20, 2013 |archive-date=September 27, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927163507/http://jfe.rochester.edu/winners.htm |url-status=dead }} ''Do liquidity measures measure liquidity?'', Volume 92, Issue 2, May 2009, Pages 153-181</ref> the Spangler-IQAM Award for the best investments paper published in the ''[[Review of Finance]]'' in 2017-2018,<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://revfin.org/awards/|title=Awards – Review of Finance|website=revfin.org|language=en|access-date=2019-07-10}}</ref> and the Philip Brown Prize for the best paper published in 2017 using SIRCA data. 
His research has been cited more than 4,300 times.<ref>{{cite web|title=Craig W. Holden Google Scholar Citations |work=Google Scholar|url=https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=TCoZzjkAAAAJ|access-date=21 November 2014}}</ref> He has written two books on financial modeling in Excel: ''Excel Modeling in Investments'' and ''Excel Modeling in Corporate Finance.''<ref>{{cite web |title= Information about Holden Excel modeling books|url=http://www.excelmodeling.com/|access-date=24 November 2014}}</ref> 
He has chaired 22 dissertations, been a member or chair of 62 dissertations,<ref>{{cite web|title=Doctoral Program Placements, Including Chair |work=Kelley School of Business Doctoral Program Placements|url=https://kelley.iu.edu/faculty-research/departments/finance/phd/placements/index.cshtml|access-date=21 November 2014}}</ref> and serves on the program committees of the Western Finance Association<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://westernfinance.org/wp-content/uploads/2016-links.pdf|title=Western Finance Association - 2016 Program}}</ref> and [[European Finance Association]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.efa2016.org/program-committee-and-track-chairs|title=Program Committee and Track Chairs {{!}} European Finance Association Conference|website=www.efa2016.org|language=en|access-date=2017-01-20}}</ref> 
He was secretary-treasurer of the [[Society for Financial Studies]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Society for Financial Studies |url=http://www.sfs.org/|access-date=21 November 2014}}</ref> He was an associate editor of the Journal of Financial Markets.<ref>{{cite book|title=Journal of Financial Markets Editorial Board|work= Elsevier  |url=http://www.journals.elsevier.com/journal-of-financial-markets/editorial-board/|access-date=21 November 2014}}</ref> 

== Research ==
Holden's research has appeared in leading journals, including the ''[[Journal of Finance]]'',<ref>{{cite journal|last=Holden|first=Craig W.|author2=Jacobsen, Stacey |title=Liquidity Measurement Problems in Fast, Competitive Markets: Expensive and Cheap Solutions|journal=Journal of Finance|year=2014|volume=69|issue=4|pages=1747–1785|doi=10.1111/jofi.12127|hdl=10.1111/jofi.12127|hdl-access=free}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal| last=Holden |first=Craig W.|author2= Avanidhar Subrahmanyam |title= Long-Lived Private Information and Imperfect Competition |journal=Journal of Finance|year=1992|volume=47|pages=247–270|doi=10.1111/j.1540-6261.1992.tb03985.x}}</ref> ''[[Journal of Financial Economics]]'',<ref>{{cite journal|last= Goyenko |first=Ruslan|author2= Craig W. Holden |author3= Charles A. Trzcinka |title= Do Liquidity Measures Measure Liquidity? |journal=Journal of Financial Economics |year=2009|volume=92|issue=2|pages=151–181|doi=10.1016/j.jfineco.2008.06.002}}</ref> ''[[Review of Financial Studies]]'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Holden |first=Craig W.|author2= Avanidhar Subrahmanyam |title= Risk Aversion, Liquidity, and Endogenous Short Horizons |journal=Review of Financial Studies |year=1996|volume=9|issue=2|pages=691–722|doi=10.1093/rfs/9.2.691}}</ref> ''[[Review of Finance]]'',<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Fong|first1=Kingsley Y. L.|last2=Holden|first2=Craig W.|last3=Trzcinka|first3=Charles A.|date=2017|title=What Are the Best Liquidity Proxies for Global Research?|journal=Review of Finance|volume=21|issue=4|pages=1355–1401|doi=10.1093/rof/rfx003|doi-access=free}}</ref> ''[[Journal of Business]]'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Holden |first=Craig W.|author2= Avanidhar Subrahmanyam |title= News Events, Information Acquisition, and Stock Price Behavior |journal=Journal of Business |year=2002|volume=75|issue=1|pages=1–32| jstor=10.1086/323503 |doi=10.1086/323503|citeseerx=10.1.1.34.4426|s2cid=153412427 }}</ref> ''Journal of Financial Markets'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Holden|first=Craig W. |title= New Low-Frequency Spread Measures |journal=Journal of Financial Markets |year=2009|volume=12|issue=4 |pages=778–813|doi=10.1016/j.finmar.2009.07.003}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal| last=Battalio |first=Robert|author2= Craig W. Holden |title= A Simple Model of Payment For Order Flow, Internalization, and Total Trading Costs |journal=Journal of Financial Markets |year=2001|volume=4|pages=33–71|doi=10.1016/s1386-4181(00)00015-x}}</ref> ''Review of Corporate Finance Studies'',<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Holden|first=Craig W.|date=2017-03-01|title=Do Acceptance and Publication Times Differ Across Finance Journals?|journal=Review of Corporate Finance Studies|language=en|volume=6|pages=102–126|doi=10.1093/rcfs/cfx009|issn=2046-9128}}</ref> ''Critical Finance Review'',<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Holden|first1=Craig W.|last2=Nam|first2=Jayoung|title=Do the LCAPM Predictions Hold? Replication and Extension Evidence|journal=Critical Finance Review|language=en|volume=8|issue=1–2|pages=29–71|doi=10.1561/104.00000071|ssrn=2848373|year=2019|s2cid=197771549 }}</ref> ''Journal of Corporate Finance'',<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Holden|first1=Craig W.|last2=Kim|first2=Daniel S.|date=2017-06-01|title=Performance share plans: Valuation and empirical tests|journal=Journal of Corporate Finance|volume=44|pages=99–125|doi=10.1016/j.jcorpfin.2017.03.004}}</ref> ''Journal of Financial Intermediation'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Chakravarty |first=Sugato|author2= Craig W. Holden |title= An Integrated Model Of Market And Limit Orders |journal=Journal of Financial Intermediation |year=1995|volume=4|issue=3|pages=213–241|doi=10.1006/jfin.1995.1010}}</ref> ''Journal of Empirical Finance'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Holden|first=Craig W.|author2= Leonard L. Lundstrum |title= Costly Trading, Managerial Myopia, and Long-Term Investment |journal=Journal of Empirical Finance |year=2009|volume=16|pages=126–135|doi=10.1016/j.jempfin.2008.05.001}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal| last=Ellul |first=Andrew|author2= Craig W. Holden|author3= Pankaj Jain |author4= Robert Jennings |title= Order Dynamics: Recent Evidence from the NYSE |journal=Journal of Empirical Finance |year=2007|volume=14|issue=5|pages=636–661|doi=10.1016/j.jempfin.2007.02.002}}</ref> ''Foundations and Trends in Finance'',<ref>{{cite journal|last=Holden|first=Craig W.|author2= Stacey Jacobsen|author3= Avanidhar Subrahmanyam |title= The Empirical Analysis of Liquidity |journal= Foundations and Trends in Finance |year=2014|volume=8|issue=4|pages=263–365|doi=10.1561/0500000044|doi-access=free}}</ref> ''[[Financial Analysts Journal]]'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Holden |first=Craig W. |title= Index Arbitrage and The Media |journal=Financial Analysts Journal|year=1991|volume=47|issue=5|pages=8–9|jstor=4479464}}</ref> ''Journal of Business Finance and Accounting'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Holden|first=Craig W.|author2= Pamela S. Stuerke |title= The Frequency of Financial Analysts' Forecast Revisions: Theory and Evidence about Determinants of Demand for Predisclosure Information |journal=Journal of Business Finance and Accounting |year=2008|volume=35|issue=7–8|pages=860–888|doi=10.1111/j.1468-5957.2008.02108.x|s2cid=154641724}}</ref> ''Mathematical Finance'',<ref>{{cite journal| last=Bagnoli |first=Mark|author2= S. Viswanathan |author3= Craig W. Holden |title= On The Existence of Linear Equilibria in Models of Market Making |journal=Mathematical Finance |year=2001|volume=11|pages=1–31|doi=10.1111/1467-9965.00106|s2cid=33081810 }}</ref> and [[Management Science (journal)|''Management Science'']].<ref>{{cite journal|last= Bhattacharya |first=Utpal|author2= Craig W. Holden |author3= Stacey Jacobsen |title= Penny Wise, Dollar Foolish: Buy-Sell Imbalances On and Around Round Numbers|journal=Management Science |year=2012|volume=15|issue=2|pages=413–431|doi=10.1287/mnsc.1110.1364|s2cid=13044134 }}</ref> His working papers are posted on the [[Social Science Research Network]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Craig W. Holden's SSRN Author Page|url=https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=32969 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Holden|first=Craig W.|date=2015-04-28|title=A Theory of Optimal Institutional Trading|ssrn=2470280}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Fong|first1=Kingsley|last2=Holden|first2=Craig|last3=Tobek|first3=Ondrej|date=2017-07-24|title=Are Volatility Over Volume Liquidity Proxies Useful For Global Or US Research?|ssrn=2989367}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Holden|first1=Craig W.|last2=Mao|first2=Yifei|last3=Nam|first3=Jayoung|date=2018-06-10|title=Price Discovery in the Stock, OTC Corporate Bond, and NYSE Corporate Bond Markets|language=en|location=Rochester, NY|ssrn=3193653}}</ref> His research has received attention in the ''[[Financial Times]]''.<ref>{{cite web|last=Authers|first=John|title=Selling Shares Like Toothpaste on 1/28/2009|url=https://www.ft.com/content/d68ff082-ed63-11dd-bd60-0000779fd2ac|work=Financial Times|access-date=21 November 2014}}</ref> and other media. He is best known for the following publications:

* Kingsley Y.L. Fong, Craig W. Holden, and Charles A. Trzcinka, 2017, What Are The Best Liquidity Proxies For Global Research?, ''Review of Finance 21'', 1355-1401.
* Craig W. Holden and Stacey Jacobsen, 2014, Liquidity Measurement Problems in Fast, Competitive Markets: Expensive and Cheap Solutions, ''[[Journal of Finance]]'' 69, 1747-1785.
* Craig W. Holden, Stacey Jacobsen, and Avanidhar Subrahmanyam, 2014, The Empirical Analysis of Liquidity, ''Foundations and Trends in Finance 8'', No. 4, 263-365.
* [[Utpal Bhattacharya]], Craig W. Holden, and Stacey Jacobsen, 2012, Penny Wise, Dollar Foolish: Buy-Sell Imbalances On and Around Round Numbers, ''[[Management Science (journal)|Management Science]]'' 15, 413-431.
* Ruslan Goyenko, Craig W. Holden, and Charles A. Trzcinka, 2009, Do Liquidity Measures Measure Liquidity?, ''[[Journal of Financial Economics]]'' 92, 153-181.
* Craig W. Holden and Avanidhar Subrahmanyam, 2002, News Events, Information Acquisition, and Stock Price Behavior, ''[[Journal of Business]]'' 75, 1-32.
* Craig W. Holden and Avanidhar Subrahmanyam, 1996, Risk Aversion, Liquidity, and Endogenous Short Horizons, ''[[Review of Financial Studies]]'' 9, 691-722.
* Craig W. Holden and Avanidhar Subrahmanyam, 1992, Long-Lived Private Information and Imperfect Competition, ''[[Journal of Finance]]'' 47, 247-270.

== Teaching ==
Holden's book, ''Excel Modeling in Investments'' (Fifth Edition; {{ISBN|9780205987245}}) teaches students how to build investments models in Excel. It covers fixed income, portfolio management, security analysis, asset pricing, international investments, options, futures, and other derivatives.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pearsonhighered.com/program/Holden-Excel-Modeling-in-Investments-5th-Edition/PGM321478.html|title=Holden, Excel Modeling in Investments, 5th Edition|website=www.pearsonhighered.com|language=en|access-date=2017-01-20}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title= Online resources for Holden Excel Modeling books|url=http://www.pearsonhighered.com/holden/|work=Pearson|access-date=24 November 2014}}</ref> His book, ''Excel Modeling in Corporate Finance'' (Fifth Edition; {{ISBN|9780205987252 }}) teaches students how to build corporate finance models in Excel. It covers time value of money, firm and project valuation, [[capital structure]], capital budgeting, financial planning, and real options.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pearsonhighered.com/program/Holden-Excel-Modeling-in-Corporate-Finance-5th-Edition/PGM192901.html|title=Holden, Excel Modeling in Corporate Finance, 5th Edition|website=www.pearsonhighered.com|language=en|access-date=2017-01-20}}</ref> His Excel modeling books have been translated into Chinese and Italian.<ref>{{cite book |title= Excel Modeling and Estimation in Investments, Third Edition, Chinese Simplified | year = 2010| publisher = 机械工业出版社| isbn = 978-7-111-30587-3|url= http://www.cmpedu.com/book/book!webDetails.do?book_id=55089|access-date=24 November 2014}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://lib.gdufe.edu.cn/opac/item.php?marc_no=0000477048|title=Excel Modeling and Estimation in the Fundamentals of Corporate Finance, Third Edition, Chinese Simplified|website=lib.gdufe.edu.cn|access-date=2017-01-20}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title= Esercizi di finanza aziendale con Excel | isbn = 978-8871923529|url=http://www.iberlibro.com/9788871923529/Esercizi-finanza-aziendale-Excel-CD-ROM-8871923529/plp|access-date=24 November 2014| last1 = Holden| first1 = Craig W.| year = 2007| publisher = Pearson}}</ref>

He has published papers about teaching in ''FEN Educator''<ref>{{cite SSRN | last=Holden |first=Craig W. |author2=Kent L. Womack|title= Spreadsheet Modeling in Finance and Investment Courses |ssrn= 241708|date=2000-09-12 }}</ref> and the ''Journal of Financial Education''<ref>{{cite journal | last=Holden |first=Craig W. |title= Save Diversification From The CAPM Controversy! An Excel-based Interactive Optimizer To Teach Diversification, Exploiting Mispriced Assets, and Asset Classes |journal=Journal of Financial Education |volume=24 |pages=49–57 |jstor=41948280 |year=1998 }}</ref>
having created three courses in [[market microstructure]] at [[Indiana University]]: 
* F335 Security Trading and Market Making at the undergraduate level<ref>{{cite web|title=F335 Security Trading and Market Making|url=http://www.kelley.iu.edu/Finance/Undergrad/CourseDescriptions/page13183.cfm?ID=9316|work=Kelley School of Business Undergraduate Program Course Descriptions|access-date=21 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141007235548/http://kelley.iu.edu/Finance/Undergrad/CourseDescriptions/page13183.cfm?ID=9316|archive-date=7 October 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>
* F535 Security Trading and Market Making at the M.B.A. level<ref>{{cite web|title=F535 Security Trading and Market Making|url=http://www.kelley.iu.edu/Finance/Masters/CourseDescriptions/page13184.cfm?ID=9518|work=Kelley School of Business MBA Program Course Descriptions|access-date=21 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140305055709/http://kelley.iu.edu/Finance/Masters/CourseDescriptions/page13184.cfm?ID=9518|archive-date=5 March 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>
* F635 Market Microstructure at the doctoral level.<ref>{{cite web|title=F635 Market Microstructure|url=http://www.kelley.iu.edu/Finance/Doctoral/CourseDescriptions/page13185.cfm?ID=10900|work=Kelley School of Business Doctoral Program Course Descriptions|access-date=21 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141011082214/http://www.kelley.iu.edu/Finance/Doctoral/CourseDescriptions/page13185.cfm?ID=10900|archive-date=11 October 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>
